title: ranunculus altitorus
intro: Ranunculus altitorus, also known as alpine buttercup or alpine ranunculus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae, characterized by its delicate white and yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides. Consuming the plant is believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating joint pain, and promoting wound healing. Ranunculus altitorus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and side effects.
